vegetables.name: Carrots
vegetables.notes: Sow direct into fine soil. Protect early sowings with fleece. Avoid stony ground.

vegetables.name: Parsnips
vegetables.notes: Sow direct. Very slow to germinate (3-4 weeks). Flavour improves after frost.

vegetables.name: Tomatoes
vegetables.notes: Best grown under cover in NE Scotland — greenhouse or polytunnel. Outdoor growing risky.

vegetables.name: Peppers
vegetables.notes: Need warmth — greenhouse essential in Aberdeen. Start early for best results.

vegetables.name: Courgettes
vegetables.notes: Very productive — 2-3 plants is usually plenty! Harvest when small for best flavour.

vegetables.name: Potatoes
vegetables.notes: Chit from Feb on a bright windowsill. Plant first earlies late March, maincrop in May. Earth up as they grow.

vegetables.name: Onions
vegetables.notes: Sets are easiest. Push into soil so tips just showing. Bend tops over in July to help ripening.

vegetables.name: Sweetcorn
vegetables.notes: Needs the warmest, most sheltered spot. Plant in blocks (not rows) for wind pollination. Challenging in NE Scotland!

vegetables.name: Lettuce
vegetables.notes: Succession sow every 2-3 weeks for continuous harvest. Tolerates partial shade. Quick to grow!

vegetables.name: Broccoli
vegetables.notes: Purple sprouting broccoli is ideal for Scotland — overwinters and harvests Feb-Apr. Net against pigeons!

vegetables.name: Kale
vegetables.notes: Ultra-hardy — grows through Scottish winter! Pick leaves from the bottom up. Net against pigeons.

vegetables.name: Peas
vegetables.notes: Sow direct. Provide support with sticks or netting. Pick regularly for more pods.

vegetables.name: Broad Beans
vegetables.notes: Very hardy — one of the earliest crops to sow. Pinch out tips when first pods form to deter blackfly.

vegetables.name: Beetroot
vegetables.notes: Sow direct. Soak seeds overnight for faster germination. Harvest golf-ball size for sweetest flavour.

vegetables.name: Leeks
vegetables.notes: Transplant into deep holes and water in — don't fill the hole. Very winter-hardy, harvest as needed.

vegetables.name: Turnips & Swede
vegetables.notes: Traditional Scottish crop! Swede needs longer growing season. Both store well over winter.
